article:
Chunk # 0.
A Microcalorimetric Study 
of the Effect of La3+on Mitochondria
Isolated from Star-Cross 288
Chicken Liver Tissue Cells
LIUYI,*,1LIUPENG,1DENGFENGJIAO ,2CHENZHONG ,1
WANGCUNXIN ,1ANDQUSONGSHENG1
1Department of Chemistry, College of Chemistry and Molecular
Sciences, Wuhan University, Wuhan 430072, People’s Republic
of China; and 2College of Life Sciences, Wuhan University,
Wuhan 430072, People’s Republic of China
Received April 10, 2001; Revised May 3, 2001; Accepted May 21, 2001
ABSTRACT
The thermogenic curves of the metabolism of mitochondria isolated
from the liver of chicken Star-Cross 288 and the effect of La3+on it were
studied by using an LKB-2277 BioActivity Monitor, ampoule method, at37°C After isolation from the chicken liver tissue, mitochondria still have
metabolic activity and can live for a long time by using the stored nutri-
ents From the thermogenic curves, we obtained the thermokinetic equa-tions under different condition The kinetic study shows that La
3+has
changed the metabolism completely Index Entries: Mitochondria; microcalorimetry; thermokinetics; ther-
mochemistry; La3+ INTRODUCTION
Mitochondria are important organelle involved in ATP production,
and even after isolation from cells, they remain capable of active metabo-lism with release of much heat They are often referred as the “power sta-tion” of the cell ATP may be produced (by the oxidation of variety ofelementary substrates) within the mitochondria present in virtually allplant and animal cells, so the study of mitochondria is very important and

*Author to whom all correspondence and reprint requests should be addressed.significant The microcalorimetric method is a simple and straightforward
method for the study of mitochondria and can obtain much importantinformation (1) The rare earth elements were characterized with their physiological
functions and biological effects on some organisms China has rich rareearth element resources; the rare earth fertility is used widely in China Itreaches almost every corner of the Chinese mainland In recent years,
growing concern has been expressed about rare earth elements because oftheir possible effects on the environment and threats to human health (2) In this article, the thermogenic curves of the metabolism of mitochon-
dria isolated from chicken Star-Cross 288 liver tissue and the effect of La
3+
on it were studied using a LKB-2277 BioActivity Monitor We find thatthere are obvious differences among their metabolic thermogenic curves.Compared to the metabolic thermogenic curves, we obtained many inter-esting results All of the experimental results are very important and sig-nificant to the study of metabolism of mitochondria and the bioeffect ofrare earth elements EXPERIMENTAL
Equipment
An LKB-2277 BioActivity Monitor (Thermometric AB, Sweden) was
used; the performance of this and the details of its construction have beendescribed previously (3–5) Materials
The Animal Room at Wuhan University provided the Star-Cross 288
chicken The isolating medium was 0.25 mol/dm3sucrose, 1 ×10–3
mol/dm3EDTA, and 1 ×10–2mol/dm3Tris-HCl, pH=7.4 The La 2O3was roasted and weighed 1 g Then, it changed into LaCl 3
with HCl and made into a 1-L solution with sterile deionized water Thus,the concentration of La
3+was 3.07 ×10–3mol/dm3 Isolation of Mitochondria
Mitochondria were isolated by first removing the liver tissue from the
chicken and washing with steriled isolating medium The liver weighed 2g, cut into small pieces, homogenized, and centrifuge at 900 gfor 10 min;
the clear superment was centrifuged twice for 20 min at 900 geach time,
the sediment being discarded after each step The clear liquid was thencentrifuged twice at high speed (12,000 g) for 20 min each time, to deposit
the mitochondria as sediment This was resuspended in the 1-mL isolatingmedium for calorimetric measurements All of the above operations wereperformed aseptically at 273–277 K.104 Yi et al Biological Trace Element Research Vol 84, 2001Experimental Determination
The metabolic thermogenic curves of mitochondria were recorded
using the ampoule method One sealed ampoule contained a referencesolution, such as the isolating medium; the other ampoule contained thesample (suspension of mitochondria) The sample normally occupied posi-tion A in the monitor and the reference occupied position B Each ampoulecontained a 1-mL sample (contained mitochondria of 2.0 g liver) or refer-ence and 2 mL of air The temperature of all of the calorimetric experiments was 37.00 °C;
the amplifier of the monitor was set at 100 µW or 300 µW RESULTS AND DISCUSSION
Thermogenic Curves
The metabolic thermogenic curve of Star-Cross 288 chicken liver mito-
chondria is shown in Fig 1 When the mitochondria were isolated, different amounts of La3+solu-
tion were added into the suspension of mitochondria, and then the meta-bolic thermogenic curves were determined The metabolic thermogeniccurves are shown in Fig 2 Thermokinetics
From Figs 1 and 2, we can see that the heat power declined exponen-
tially So, we can calculate the decline rate constant of metabolism, kd, fromLa in Chicken Liver Cells 105
Biological Trace Element Research Vol 84, 2001

mitochondria.protocol:
**Title: A Microcalorimetric Study of the Effect of La3+ on Mitochondria Isolated from Star-Cross 288 Chicken Liver Tissue Cells**

**Abstract:**
- The study investigates the effect of La3+ on the metabolism of mitochondria isolated from the liver of chicken Star-Cross 288.
- Mitochondria, even after isolation, retain metabolic activity and can survive using stored nutrients.
- The thermogenic curves of mitochondrial metabolism were obtained under different conditions.
- The study shows that La3+ completely changes the metabolism.

**Introduction:**
- Mitochondria are organelles involved in ATP production and remain metabolically active even after isolation from cells.
- They are often referred to as the "power station" of the cell.
- The study of mitochondria is important due to their role in ATP production.
- Microcalorimetry is a simple and straightforward method for studying mitochondria and obtaining important information.
- Rare earth elements, including La3+, have physiological functions and biological effects on organisms.
- China has rich rare earth element resources, and their use is widespread.
- Concerns have been raised about the effects of rare earth elements on the environment and human health.

**Experimental:**
- Equipment: An LKB-2277 BioActivity Monitor was used.
- Materials: Star-Cross 288 chicken liver, isolating medium (0.25 mol/dm3 sucrose, 1 × 10–3 mol/dm3 EDTA, 1 × 10–2 mol/dm3 Tris-HCl, pH=7.4), La2O3, HCl, sterile deionized water.
- La3+ concentration: 3.07 × 10–3 mol/dm3.

**Isolation of Mitochondria:**
1. Remove the liver tissue from the chicken and wash it with sterile isolating medium.
2. Cut the liver into small pieces and homogenize.
3. Centrifuge the homogenate at 900 g for 10 min.
4. Discard the supernatant and centrifuge the clear liquid twice at 900 g for 20 min each time.
5. Centrifuge the clear liquid at high speed (12,000 g) twice for 20 min each time to deposit the mitochondria as sediment.
6. Resuspend the sediment in 1 mL of isolating medium for calorimetric measurements.
7. All isolation steps were performed aseptically at 273-277 K.

**Experimental Determination:**
- Metabolic thermogenic curves of mitochondria were recorded using the ampoule method.
- One sealed ampoule contained the reference solution (isolating medium), and the other ampoule contained the sample (suspension of mitochondria).
- Each ampoule contained 1 mL of sample or reference and 2 mL of air.
- The temperature of the experiments was 37.00 °C.
- The amplifier of the monitor was set at 100 µW or 300 µW.

**Results and Discussion:**
- Thermogenic Curves: The metabolic thermogenic curve of Star-Cross 288 chicken liver mitochondria is shown in Fig 1.
- Different amounts of La3+ solution were added to the mitochondria suspension, and the metabolic thermogenic curves were determined (Fig 2).
- Thermokinetics: The heat power declined exponentially, allowing calculation of the decline rate constant of metabolism (kd).

**Conclusion:**
The article provides a detailed protocol for isolating mitochondria from chicken liver tissue. The steps include tissue removal, washing, homogenization, and centrifugation. However, the article does not provide information on the specific centrifugation speeds or the composition of the isolating medium. The protocol also lacks details on the ampoule method used for recording thermogenic curves. Overall, the protocol is partially described, with some missing information
mitochondria.organisms:
**Organisms Mentioned in the Mitochondrial Extraction Protocol:**
- Star-Cross 288 chicken

summary:
Revised Summary (Iteration 2):
Conducted at the Department of Chemistry, College of Chemistry and Molecular Sciences, Wuhan University, China, this study investigates the effect of La3+ on mitochondria, the power stations of chicken liver tissue cells. Using an LKB-2277 BioActivity Monitor, the researchers analyzed the thermogenic curves of mitochondrial metabolism. The study reveals a complete metabolic change induced by La3+ in the mitochondria. This research contributes to our understanding of the role of mitochondria in ATP production and the bioeffect of rare earth elements, which have implications for environmental and human health.

Revised Summary (Iteration 3):
Conducted at the Department of Chemistry, College of Chemistry and Molecular Sciences, Wuhan University, China, this study utilizes an LKB-2277 BioActivity Monitor to investigate the effect of La3+ on mitochondria, the power stations of chicken liver tissue cells. The analysis of thermogenic curves reveals a complete metabolic change induced by La3+ in the mitochondria. This research enhances our understanding of the role of mitochondria in ATP production and the bioeffect of rare earth elements. It also highlights the environmental and human health implications of rare earth elements.

Revised Summary (Iteration 4):
Conducted at the Department of Chemistry, College of Chemistry and Molecular Sciences, Wuhan University, China, this study employs an LKB-2277 BioActivity Monitor to investigate the effect of La3+ on mitochondria, the power stations of chicken liver tissue cells. The analysis of thermogenic curves reveals a complete metabolic change induced by La3+ in the mitochondria. This research enhances our understanding of the role of mitochondria in ATP production and the bioeffect of rare earth elements. It also highlights the environmental and human health implications associated with rare earth elements, which are of growing concern.

Revised Summary (Iteration 5):
Conducted at the Department of Chemistry, College of Chemistry and Molecular Sciences, Wuhan University, China, this study utilizes an LKB-2277 BioActivity Monitor to investigate the effect of La3+ on mitochondria, the power stations of chicken liver tissue cells. The analysis of thermogenic curves reveals a complete metabolic change induced by La3+ in the mitochondria. This research enhances our understanding of the role of mitochondria in ATP production and the bioeffect of rare earth elements. It also highlights the environmental and human health implications associated with the widespread use of rare earth elements in China.

Revised Summary (Iteration 6):
Conducted at the Department of Chemistry, College of Chemistry and Molecular Sciences, Wuhan University, China, this study utilizes an LKB-2277 BioActivity Monitor to investigate the effect of La3+ on mitochondria, the power stations of chicken liver tissue cells. The analysis of thermogenic curves reveals a complete metabolic change induced by La3+ in the mitochondria. This research enhances our understanding of the role of mitochondria in ATP production and the bioeffect of rare earth elements. It also highlights the environmental and human health implications associated with the widespread use of rare earth elements in China, which reach almost every corner of the Chinese mainland.

mitochondria.protocol:
1. "In our experiments, we used the isolating medium to supply the osmotic pressure, without added nutrients that can be used by mitochondria."
2. "Thus, the metabolism of mitochondria is a kind of endogenous metabolism."

**Conclusion:**
The article does not provide a detailed protocol for mitochondrial isolation. It only mentions that an isolating medium was used to supply osmotic pressure without added nutrients. The focus of the article is on the metabolic thermogenic curves of liver mitochondria affected by La3+. The information provided is insufficient to replicate the mitochondrial isolation procedure.
mitochondria.organisms: No organisms mentioned in the mitochondrial extraction protocol.

summary: The metabolic thermogenic curve of Star-Cross 288 liver mitochondria was affected by the addition of La3+. The addition of La3+ increased the maximum heat production power (Pm) and the decline rate constants (kd). There is a linear relationship between Pm and the concentration of La3+, as well as between kd and the concentration of La3+. The thermokinetic equation for mitochondria metabolism remained unchanged with the addition of La3+, but the heat power declined faster and kd increased with increasing La3+ concentration. This suggests that La3+ alters the metabolic mechanism of mitochondria. Microcalorimetry is a useful tool for studying mitochondria and can provide valuable kinetic and thermodynamic information.

However, for different kinds of mitochondria, orunder different conditions, there will be different kinds of thermogeniccurve, from which we can obtain some information We will study all ofthese further Because of the high sensitivity of the monitor and the fact that the
whole metabolism of samples may be examined automatically and contin-uously, the microcalorimetric method may reveal more and even newdetails about the metabolism than the existing methods (9) The
microcalorimetric method requires only an observable difference betweenthe power production in the treated and controlled incubations; unlikemany other procedures, the microcalorimetric method does not require atransparent solution Colored or turbid solutions, even suspensions, can beput into the calorimeter (10) This approximates more closely the in vivo
state than many other techniques do The microcalorimetric method can beused in almost every aspects of biological sciences and can obtain muchinformation that other methods cannot We can also study the kinetics andthermodynamics of biological sciences further; all of these are every sig-nificant for biological sciences.La in Chicken Liver Cells 111
Biological Trace Element Research Vol 84, 2001Table 2

summary: Different kinds of mitochondria exhibit different thermogenic curves, providing valuable information. The microcalorimetric method, unlike other techniques, can analyze the whole metabolism of samples automatically and continuously. It can be used with colored or turbid solutions, mimicking the in vivo state. This method is highly versatile and can provide insights that other methods cannot, including studying the kinetics and thermodynamics of biological sciences.

topics.topic: Microcalorimetric method for analyzing whole metabolism
topics.justification: The summary highlights the unique capabilities of the microcalorimetric method, which allows for the automatic and continuous analysis of the whole metabolism of samples. This topic emphasizes the distinct advantages of this technique compared to other methods.

The summary mentions that different kinds of mitochondria exhibit different thermogenic curves, which provide valuable information. This topic focuses on the variation in thermogenic curves and their significance in understanding mitochondrial function.

The summary states that the microcalorimetric method is highly versatile and can provide insights that other methods cannot. This topic highlights the broad applicability of the method and its potential for studying various aspects of biological sciences.

topics.topic: Analyzing colored or turbid solutions to mimic the in vivo state
topics.justification: The summary mentions that the microcalorimetric method can be used with colored or turbid solutions, allowing for the mimicking of the in vivo state. This topic emphasizes the ability of the method to simulate real-life conditions and its relevance in studying biological processes.

topics.topic: Kinetics and thermodynamics of biological sciences
topics.justification: The summary states that the microcalorimetric method can provide insights into the kinetics and thermodynamics of biological sciences. This topic highlights the specific area of research that can benefit from the application of the method.

If you were a researcher interested in studying the thermogenic curves of different types of mitochondria, how would you utilize the microcalorimetric method to gain valuable insights that other techniques cannot provide?
